reasoning: Google's 'Year in Search' highlights 'Trending' people, which measures the highest spike in search traffic in 2025 compared to the previous year. Joe Biden had massive search spikes in 2024 due to his presidential campaign, debate performance, and unprecedented withdrawal from the race. Because 'trending' requires surpassing the previous year's search volume, it is highly unlikely that his search traffic in 2025—a year he left office and entered retirement—would spike higher than his 2024 peaks. Thus, he is unlikely to even make the 2025 global trending list. Furthermore, the requirement is to land exactly at the #2 rank, which is an extremely narrow outcome. Given these factors, the probability is set at the minimum threshold (2%).
